summary: nolimit-x ships an obfuscated bulk phishing/spam toolkit with OAuth device-code abuse and contact harvesting
details: The package distributes a commercial bulk-email/SMS phishing toolkit. All 40+ JavaScript files under.ad/ are obfuscated with javascript-obfuscator (RC4 string-array, control-flow flattening, self-defending wrappers) and the package.json self-describes as 'Advanced email sender' with keywords including 'red-team' and 'smtp'. Capability surface visible after de-obfuscation includes: SMTP rotation and bulk sending, SMS dispatch, OAuth2 device-code flow against Microsoft using the Azure CLI public client_id 9e5f94bc-e8a4-4e73-b8be-63364c29d753 (a known device-code-phishing pattern abusing a first-party Microsoft client), Microsoft Graph harvesting of contacts and Sent Items, IMAP mailbox scraping for address books, and DKIM key generation..ad/x4.js additionally posts the operator's license key, generated hardware ID, hostname, and platform to http://api.nolimitent.xyz:4100 in cleartext on every CLI invocation, transmitting installer host identifiers without TLS. Installing or pulling this as a transitive dependency drags an obfuscated phishing/spam framework and a cleartext host-fingerprint beacon into the dependency tree; the obfuscation across the entire executable surface (devDependency javascript-obfuscator + build script that runs scripts/obfuscate.js) is consistent with evasion of supply-chain review rather than IP protection. The package's advertised purpose is itself an attack capability against third-party Microsoft 365 / email users.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
